speaker: KING
text: Farewell, young lords. These warlike principles

speaker: KING
text: Do not throw from you.—And you, my lords, farewell.

text: Share the advice betwixt you. If both gain all,

speaker: KING
text: The gift doth stretch itself as ’tis received

text: And is enough for both.

text: ’Tis our hope, sir,

text: After well-entered soldiers, to return

speaker:
FIRST

text: And find your Grace in health.

speaker: KING
text: No, no, it cannot be. And yet my heart

speaker: KING
text: Will not confess he owes the malady

speaker: KING
text: That doth my life besiege. Farewell, young lords.

speaker: KING
text: Whether I live or die, be you the sons

speaker: KING
text: Of worthy Frenchmen. Let higher Italy—

speaker: KING
text: Those bated that inherit but the fall

speaker: KING
text: Of the last monarchy—see that you come

speaker: KING
text: Not to woo honor but to wed it. When

text: The bravest questant shrinks, find what you seek,

speaker: KING
text: That fame may cry you loud. I say farewell.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Health at your bidding serve your Majesty!

speaker: KING
text: Those girls of Italy, take heed of them.

speaker: KING
text: They say our French lack language to deny

text: If they demand. Beware of being captives

speaker: KING
text: Before you serve.

speaker: LORDS
text: Our hearts receive your warnings.

speaker: KING
text: Farewell.—Come hither to me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: O my sweet lord, that you will stay behind us!

text: ’Tis not his fault, the spark.

              LORD
text: O, ’tis brave wars.

speaker: PAROLLES
text: Most admirable. I have seen those wars.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I am commanded here and kept a coil

speaker: BERTRAM
text: With “Too young,” and “The next year,” and “’Tis too early.”

speaker: PAROLLES
text: An thy mind stand to ’t, boy, steal away bravely.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I shall stay here the forehorse to a smock,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Creaking my shoes on the plain masonry

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Till honor be bought up, and no sword worn

text: But one to dance with. By heaven, I’ll steal away!

FIRST
               
              LORD
text: There’s honor in the theft.

text: Commit it, count.

              LORD
text: I am your accessory. And so, farewell.

text: I grow to you, and our parting is a tortured

text: body.

text: Sweet Monsieur Parolles.

text: his reports for me.

text: Stay the King.

text: of too cold an adieu. Be more expressive to them,

text: there do muster true gait; eat, speak, and move

text: under the influence of the most received star, and,

speaker: LAFEW
text: Pardon, my lord, for me and for my tidings.

speaker: KING
text: I’ll fee thee to stand up.

speaker: LAFEW
text: Then here’s a man stands that has brought his pardon.

speaker: LAFEW
text: I would you had kneeled, my lord, to ask me mercy,

speaker: LAFEW
text: And that at my bidding you could so stand up.

speaker: KING
text: I would I had, so I had broke thy pate

speaker: KING
text: And asked thee mercy for ’t.

speaker: LAFEW
text: Good faith, across.

speaker: LAFEW
text: But, my good lord, ’tis thus: will you be cured

speaker: LAFEW
text: Of your infirmity?

speaker: KING
text: No.

speaker: LAFEW
text: O, will you eat

speaker: LAFEW
text: No grapes, my royal fox? Yes, but you will

speaker: LAFEW
text: My noble grapes, an if my royal fox

speaker: LAFEW
text: Could reach them. I have seen a medicine

speaker: LAFEW
text: That’s able to breathe life into a stone,

speaker: LAFEW
text: Quicken a rock, and make you dance canary

speaker: LAFEW
text: With sprightly fire and motion, whose simple touch

speaker: LAFEW
text: Is powerful to araise King Pippen, nay,

speaker: LAFEW
text: To give great Charlemagne a pen in ’s hand

speaker: LAFEW
text: And write to her a love line.

speaker: KING
text: What “her” is this?

speaker: LAFEW
text: Why, Doctor She. My lord, there’s one arrived,

speaker: LAFEW
text: If you will see her. Now, by my faith and honor,

speaker: LAFEW
text: If seriously I may convey my thoughts

speaker: LAFEW
text: In this my light deliverance, I have spoke

speaker: LAFEW
text: With one that in her sex, her years, profession,

speaker: LAFEW
text: Wisdom, and constancy hath amazed me more

speaker: LAFEW
text: Than I dare blame my weakness. Will you see her—

speaker: LAFEW
text: For that is her demand—and know her business?

speaker: LAFEW
text: That done, laugh well at me.

text: Now, good Lafew,

text: Bring in the admiration, that we with thee

text: By wond’ring how thou took’st it.

text: Nay, I’ll fit you,

text: And not be all day neither.

text: Thus he his special nothing ever prologues.

speaker: LAFEW

speaker: KING
text: This haste hath wings indeed.

speaker: LAFEW
text: Nay, come your ways.

text: This is his Majesty. Say your mind to him.

text: A traitor you do look like, but such traitors

text: His Majesty seldom fears. I am Cressid’s uncle

speaker: LAFEW
text: That dare leave two together. Fare you well.

speaker: KING
text: Now, fair one, does your business follow us?

speaker: HELEN
text: Ay, my good lord,

speaker: HELEN
text: Gerard de Narbon was my father,

speaker: HELEN
text: In what he did profess well found.

speaker: KING
text: I knew him.

speaker: HELEN
text: The rather will I spare my praises towards him.

speaker: HELEN
text: Knowing him is enough. On ’s bed of death

speaker: HELEN
text: Many receipts he gave me, chiefly one

speaker: HELEN
text: Which, as the dearest issue of his practice,

speaker: HELEN
text: And of his old experience th’ only darling,

speaker: HELEN
text: He bade me store up as a triple eye,

speaker: HELEN
text: Safer than mine own two, more dear. I have so,

speaker: HELEN
text: And hearing your high Majesty is touched

speaker: HELEN
text: With that malignant cause wherein the honor

speaker: HELEN
text: Of my dear father’s gift stands chief in power,

speaker: HELEN
text: I come to tender it and my appliance

speaker: HELEN
text: With all bound humbleness.

speaker: KING
text: We thank you, maiden,

speaker: KING
text: But may not be so credulous of cure,

speaker: KING
text: When our most learnèd doctors leave us and

speaker: KING
text: The congregated college have concluded

speaker: KING
text: That laboring art can never ransom nature

speaker: KING
text: From her inaidible estate. I say we must not

speaker: KING
text: So stain our judgment or corrupt our hope

speaker: KING
text: To prostitute our past-cure malady

speaker: KING
text: To empirics, or to dissever so

speaker: KING
text: Our great self and our credit to esteem

speaker: KING
text: A senseless help when help past sense we deem.

speaker: HELEN
text: My duty, then, shall pay me for my pains.

speaker: HELEN
text: I will no more enforce mine office on you,

speaker: HELEN
text: Humbly entreating from your royal thoughts

speaker: HELEN
text: A modest one to bear me back again.

speaker: KING
text: I cannot give thee less, to be called grateful.

speaker: KING
text: Thou thought’st to help me, and such thanks I give

speaker: KING
text: As one near death to those that wish him live.

speaker: KING
text: But what at full I know, thou know’st no part,

speaker: KING
text: I knowing all my peril, thou no art.

speaker: HELEN
text: What I can do can do no hurt to try

speaker: HELEN
text: Since you set up your rest ’gainst remedy.

speaker: HELEN
text: He that of greatest works is finisher

speaker: HELEN
text: Oft does them by the weakest minister.

speaker: HELEN
text: So holy writ in babes hath judgment shown

speaker: HELEN
text: When judges have been babes. Great floods have flown

speaker: HELEN
text: From simple sources, and great seas have dried

speaker: HELEN
text: When miracles have by the great’st been denied.

speaker: HELEN
text: Oft expectation fails, and most oft there

speaker: HELEN
text: Where most it promises, and oft it hits

speaker: HELEN
text: Where hope is coldest and despair most shifts.

speaker: KING
text: I must not hear thee. Fare thee well, kind maid.

speaker: KING
text: Thy pains, not used, must by thyself be paid.

speaker: KING
text: Proffers not took reap thanks for their reward.

speaker: HELEN
text: Inspirèd merit so by breath is barred.

text: It is not so with Him that all things knows

text: As ’tis with us that square our guess by shows;

speaker: HELEN
text: But most it is presumption in us when

text: The help of heaven we count the act of men.

text: Dear sir, to my endeavors give consent.

speaker: HELEN
text: Of heaven, not me, make an experiment.

text: I am not an impostor that proclaim

speaker: HELEN
text: Myself against the level of mine aim,

text: But know I think and think I know most sure

text: My art is not past power nor you past cure.

text: Art thou so confident? Within what space

text: Hop’st thou my cure?

speaker: HELEN
text: The greatest grace lending grace,

text: Ere twice the horses of the sun shall bring

text: Their fiery torcher his diurnal ring;

text: Moist Hesperus hath quenched her sleepy lamp;

text: Hath told the thievish minutes, how they pass,

text: Health shall live free, and sickness freely die.

text: Upon thy certainty and confidence

text: What dar’st thou venture?

speaker: HELEN
text: Tax of impudence,

speaker: HELEN
text: A strumpet’s boldness, a divulgèd shame;

speaker: HELEN
text: Traduced by odious ballads, my maiden’s name

text: Seared otherwise; nay, worse of worst, extended

speaker: KING
text: Methinks in thee some blessèd spirit doth speak

speaker: KING
text: His powerful sound within an organ weak,

speaker: KING
text: And what impossibility would slay

speaker: KING
text: In common sense, sense saves another way.

text: Thy life is dear, for all that life can rate

text: Worth name of life in thee hath estimate:

speaker: KING

speaker: KING
text: That happiness and prime can happy call.

speaker: KING
text: Thou this to hazard needs must intimate

speaker: KING
text: Skill infinite or monstrous desperate.

speaker: KING
text: Sweet practicer, thy physic I will try,

speaker: KING
text: That ministers thine own death if I die.

speaker: HELEN
text: If I break time or flinch in property

speaker: HELEN
text: Of what I spoke, unpitied let me die,

speaker: HELEN
text: And well deserved. Not helping, death’s my fee.

text: But if I help, what do you promise me?

text: Make thy demand.

speaker: HELEN
text: But will you make it even?

speaker: KING
text: Ay, by my scepter and my hopes of heaven.

text: Then shalt thou give me with thy kingly hand

text: What husband in thy power I will command.

speaker: HELEN
text: Exempted be from me the arrogance

text: To choose from forth the royal blood of France,

speaker: HELEN
text: My low and humble name to propagate

text: With any branch or image of thy state;

text: Is free for me to ask, thee to bestow.

speaker: KING
text: Here is my hand. The premises observed,

speaker: KING
text: Thy will by my performance shall be served.

speaker: KING
text: So make the choice of thy own time, for I,

text: Thy resolved patient, on thee still rely.

text: Though more to know could not be more to trust:
